frameworks.name: probabilistic inference in the brain
frameworks.one_line: Brain implements probabilistic inference at sensory and cognitive levels.

frameworks.name: cognitively plausible approximate inference algorithms
frameworks.one_line: Psychology-derived approximation algorithms for intractable inference problems.

frameworks.name: neural circuit mechanisms for sensory inference
frameworks.one_line: Low-level inference circuits scaled up to handle complex problems.

core_question: How can neural mechanisms that solve low-level sensory probabilistic inference be extended to support complex high-level cognitive inference?
key_assumptions: High-level cognitive inference problems are intractable in exact form
Approximation is necessary for cognition-scale inference
Sensory and cognitive inference share underlying mechanisms
key_findings: A gap exists between simple sensory inference and intractable cognitive inference
Psychologists have proposed cognitively plausible approximate inference algorithms
These algorithmic accounts are increasingly being linked to neural circuit mechanisms
interpretations: Neural mechanisms for low-level sensory inference can be extended to tackle complex cognitive inference
